channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Zelenskyy leaves White House without signing minerals deal after Oval Office blow up
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump berated Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for being “disrespectful” Friday in an extraordinary Oval Office meeting, then abruptly called off the signing of a minerals deal with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What they said: Trump, Zelenskyy and Vance's heated argument in the Oval Office
item.description: President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance had a heated exchange with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in the Oval Office over the war in Ukraine, accusing him of not showing gratitude after he challenged Vance on the question of diplomacy with Russia’s Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Politics Friday: Newly elected lawmakers reflect on their first legislative session
item.description: MPR News host Brian Bakst talks with two state representatives serving in their first legislative session. Later, a conversation with State Auditor Julie Blaha.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Bloodthirsty Venezuelan gang put on notice as new bill curbs blue state sanctuary policies
item.description: Rep. Gabe Evans, R-Colo., introduced the "Unhandcuffing Police to Locate and Interdict Foreign Transgressors Act" earlier this week to prompt public officials in Colorado to communicate with feds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House touts extradition of cartel figures amid Trump admin crackdown
item.description: White House touts the extradition of cartel figures, as the Trump administration seeks to crack down on drug cartels and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Vance go off on Zelensky in contentious Oval Office spat
item.description: President Trump went off on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during an extraordinary Oval Office meeting in front of cameras on Friday in which he raised his voice and called the foreign leader “disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas leaders defend Black official after lawmaker’s public interrogation of DEI policies left her in tears
item.description: The topic was a budget request from a state agency. The exchange grew emotional when a lawmaker pressed for answers about a strategic plan that praises diversity.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Alejandra Martinez, KPRC 2

item.title: Most Americans who experienced severe winter weather see climate change at work, AP-NORC poll shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Matt Ries has lived in Florida only three years, but everyone told him last summer was unusually hot. That was followed by three hurricanes in close succession. Then temperatures dropped below freezing for days this winter, and snow blanketed part of the state.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and AMELIA THOMSON-DeVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Those with gender dysphoria can no longer serve in military: Pentagon
item.description: (NewsNation) — A memo sent to Defense Department leaders Thursday states those with gender dysphoria are no longer eligible for military service. This document, written by Darin S.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers will get a new email demanding their accomplishments, with a key change
item.description: Federal employees should expect another email on Saturday requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to sign order Friday designating English as the official language of the US
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order on Friday designating English as the official language of the United States, according to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Commissioner Ford Jr. charged with bribery, tax evasion
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Shelby County Commissioner Edmund Ford Jr. was taken into federal custody after turning himself in Friday morning. He is charged with bribery and kickbacks concerning federal funds and six counts of attempted tax evasion, according to a federal indictment.
item.dc:creator: Katie Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: Prince Harry's immigration status under fire over drugs, possible lies
item.description: Prince Harry's immigration status has come under fire after he admitted to recreational use of marijuana, cocaine and psychedelics.
item.dc:creator: Tom Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: I 'wouldn't be opposed' to using private forces for deportations
item.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday that he "wouldn't be opposed" to using private forces to assist with deportations.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump’s crackdown plays into misperceptions about immigrants and crime
item.description: The White House portrays its immigration crackdown as a success. Critics say the administration is trying to look tough for the cameras, and worry about the “tail wagging the dog.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: NIH partially lifts freeze on funding process for medical research
item.description: Thousands of grant applications had been stalled when the Trump administration blocked the National Institutes of Health from posting notices to the Federal Register.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats Cortez Masto, Rosen join Republicans in scrubbing Biden-era drilling rule
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen joined their Republican colleagues in voting to reverse a Biden-era policy related to offshore drilling.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt change as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
